questions.question: True or False — An atom is the basic unit of matter.
True or False — An atom is the smallest particle of an element capable of independent existence.
questions.answer: False. An atom is generally not capable of independent existence. It is the smallest particle of an element that takes part in a chemical reaction.

questions.question: True or False — An element is made up of one or more kinds of atoms.
questions.answer: False. An element is made up of only one kind of atoms.

questions.question: True or False — Properties of an element depend upon atoms constituting it.
True or False — Sulphur is made of only one type of atoms.
True or False — A molecule is not capable of independent existence.
questions.answer: False. A molecule is the smallest particle of a substance (element or compound) that is capable of independent existence.

questions.question: True or False — A radical is always a single atom with a charge.
questions.answer: False. A radical may be a single atom or a group of atoms that behaves as a single unit and carries a charge.

questions.question: In the atom diagram, choose the correct statement about X (the central part of the atom).
questions.answer: X is the nucleus. It contains protons (positively charged) and neutrons (neutral/no charge).

questions.question: In the atom diagram, choose the correct statement about Y (the paths around the nucleus).
questions.answer: Y is a shell (or orbit) where electrons revolve around the nucleus.

questions.question: Choose the correct statement about the particle count in the shown atom.
questions.answer: It contains nine protons and ten neutrons in its nucleus.

questions.question: Choose the correct statement about subatomic particles.
questions.answer: Protons, electrons and neutrons are the three subatomic particles of an atom.

questions.question: Atom Z has 11 protons and 12 neutrons. What is its atomic number?
Atom R has 19 protons and 20 neutrons. How many electrons does a neutral atom R have?
questions.answer: 19 (In a neutral atom, number of electrons = number of protons)

questions.question: In the modern periodic table, elements are arranged in increasing order of atomic _____.
The atomic number of an element equals the number of which subatomic particles?
questions.answer: Protons (which also equals the number of electrons in a neutral atom)

questions.question: Nitrogen has an atomic number of 7. State the number of protons and electrons.
Complete the electron distribution for nitrogen (atomic number 7).
questions.answer: Nucleus: 7 protons and 7 neutrons. Electron arrangement: 2 electrons in first shell, 5 electrons in second shell (2, 5).

questions.question: Element X has 3 protons. It is placed at what position in the periodic table?
According to Dalton, an atom is divisible or indivisible?
questions.answer: Indivisible (as per Dalton's atomic theory; later proven wrong by discovery of subatomic particles)

questions.question: Name the discoverers of the electron, proton and neutron.
questions.answer: Electron — J.J. Thomson, Proton — Goldstein (discovered canal rays) / Rutherford (named it), Neutron — James Chadwick

questions.question: State the molecular formula and atomicity of fluorine.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of chlorine.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of bromine.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of iodine.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of hydrogen.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of oxygen.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of nitrogen.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of helium.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of neon.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of argon.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of ozone.
State the molecular formula and atomicity of phosphorus.
Carbon and oxygen combine to form which compound molecule?
Sulphur and oxygen combine to form which compound molecule?
Nitrogen and oxygen combine to form which compound molecule?
Nitrogen and hydrogen combine to form which compound molecule?
Hydrogen and chlorine combine to form which compound molecule?
Complete the definition: A radical is a _____ atom or _____ of atoms behaving as a _____ unit and carrying a _____.
questions.answer: A radical is a single atom or group of atoms behaving as a single unit and carrying a charge.

questions.question: State the group of atoms in the ammonium radical (NH₄).
State the group of atoms in the bicarbonate radical (HCO₃).
questions.answer: 1 hydrogen atom, 1 carbon atom and 3 oxygen atoms

questions.question: State the group of atoms in the bisulphite radical (HSO₃).
questions.answer: 1 hydrogen atom, 1 sulphur atom and 3 oxygen atoms

questions.question: State the group of atoms in the bisulphate radical (HSO₄).
questions.answer: 1 hydrogen atom, 1 sulphur atom and 4 oxygen atoms

questions.question: State the group of atoms in the nitrate radical (NO₃).
State the group of atoms in the hydroxide radical (OH).
State the group of atoms in the carbonate radical (CO₃).
State the group of atoms in the sulphite radical (SO₃).
State the group of atoms in the sulphate radical (SO₄).
State the group of atoms in the phosphate radical (PO₄).
Differentiate between a basic radical and an acid radical.
questions.answer: A basic radical is positively charged (cation), e.g. Na⁺, NH₄⁺. An acid radical is negatively charged (anion), e.g. SO₄²⁻, NO₃⁻.

questions.question: Differentiate between a cation and an anion.
questions.answer: A cation has a positive charge (formed by losing electrons), e.g. Na⁺, Ca²⁺. An anion has a negative charge (formed by gaining electrons), e.g. Cl⁻, O²⁻.

questions.question: Complete: Valency is the number of _____ atoms that combine with or displace one atom of an element/radical.
From HCl, H₂SO₄, NH₃, CH₄ — state the valency of Cl, SO₄, N, and C.
In ZnO, what is the valency of zinc?
questions.answer: 2 (Oxygen has valency 2, so zinc must also have valency 2 to balance)

questions.question: Are valencies of metals positive or negative? And non-metals?
questions.answer: Metals have positive valency; non-metals have negative valency.

questions.question: What is the valency of monovalent metals?
What is the valency of divalent metals?
Give an example of a monovalent metal.
Give an example of a divalent metal.
Give an example of a trivalent metal.
Name elements with variable valencies: valency 1 and 2; valency 2 and 3; valency 2 and 4.
questions.answer: Copper (Cu) — valency 1 and 2; Iron (Fe) — valency 2 and 3; Lead (Pb) — valency 2 and 4

questions.question: Write the chemical formula of sodium chloride.
questions.answer: NaCl (Na⁺ and Cl⁻ — valencies balance as 1:1)

questions.question: Write the chemical formula of potassium bromide.
questions.answer: KBr (K⁺ and Br⁻ — valencies balance as 1:1)

questions.question: Write the chemical formula of sodium iodide.
questions.answer: NaI (Na⁺ and I⁻ — valencies balance as 1:1)

questions.question: Write the chemical formula of zinc chloride.
Write the chemical formula of ammonium sulphate.
Write the chemical formula of calcium bisulphate.
Write the chemical formula of iron(III) chloride.
Write the chemical formula of copper(I) oxide.
Write the chemical formula of lead(II) nitrate.
Write the chemical formula of aluminium hydroxide.
Write the chemical formula of sodium bicarbonate.
questions.answer: NaHCO₃ (Na⁺ and HCO₃⁻ — valencies balance as 1:1)

questions.question: Write the chemical formula of calcium phosphate.
Write the chemical formula of sulphuric acid.
Write the chemical formula of iron(III) sulphate.
In the reaction: Zinc + Dilute sulphuric acid → Zinc sulphate + Hydrogen — name the reactants.
In the reaction: Zinc + Dilute sulphuric acid → Zinc sulphate + Hydrogen — name the products.
Define reactants and products.
questions.answer: Reactants are the starting substances that undergo a chemical change. Products are the new substances formed as a result of the chemical reaction.

questions.question: What does the arrow (→) indicate in a chemical equation?
questions.answer: It means "gives" or "yields" or "forms" — indicating the direction of the reaction from reactants to products.

questions.question: Is the equation written using names (Zinc + Dilute sulphuric acid → ...) a word equation or molecular equation?
Write the molecular equation for: Zinc + Dilute sulphuric acid → Zinc sulphate + Hydrogen
What do the symbols (s), (dil.), and (g) mean in chemical equations?
For N₂ + 3H₂ ⇌ 2NH₃ — name reactants, products, states, and what does ⇌ mean?
questions.answer: Reactants: Nitrogen (N₂) and Hydrogen (H₂). Product: Ammonia (NH₃). All are gases. The double arrow (⇌) indicates a reversible reaction.

questions.question: In Fe + H₂SO₄ → FeSO₄ + H₂, are the atoms of each element equal on both sides?
Is the equation Fe + H₂SO₄ → FeSO₄ + H₂ balanced or unbalanced?
In Cu + HCl → CuCl₂ + H₂, are the atoms of each element equal on both sides?
questions.answer: No. Cu has 1 on each side, but Cl has 1 on left and 2 on right, H has 1 on left and 2 on right.

questions.question: Is Cu + HCl → CuCl₂ + H₂ balanced? If not, write the balanced equation.
questions.answer: Unbalanced. Balanced equation: Cu + 2HCl → CuCl₂ + H₂

questions.question: Why should a chemical equation be balanced? State the relevant law.
questions.answer: A chemical equation must be balanced to obey the Law of Conservation of Mass, which states that matter can neither be created nor destroyed — the total mass of reactants must equal the total mass of products.

questions.question: List the first 20 elements with their symbols, atomic numbers and type (metal/non-metal/noble gas/metalloid).
questions.answer: H(1)-non-metal, He(2)-noble gas, Li(3)-metal, Be(4)-metal, B(5)-metalloid, C(6)-non-metal, N(7)-non-metal, O(8)-non-metal, F(9)-non-metal, Ne(10)-noble gas, Na(11)-metal, Mg(12)-metal, Al(13)-metal, Si(14)-metalloid, P(15)-non-metal, S(16)-non-metal, Cl(17)-non-metal, Ar(18)-noble gas, K(19)-metal, Ca(20)-metal

questions.question: The modern periodic table arranges elements in ascending order of atomic _____.
How many horizontal rows (periods) are there in the modern periodic table?
The group number signifies the number of _____ in the outermost shell.
Elements in the same group have the same/different valency and similar _____ properties.
questions.answer: Same valency and similar chemical properties

questions.question: Name three Group 1 reactive metals with atomic numbers between 1 and 20.
What is the valency of Group 2 elements? Name three Group 2 elements (atomic number 1-20).
Fluorine (F), Chlorine (Cl) and Bromine (Br) belong to which group? What type of elements are they?
Group 18 contains what type of gases? Name three examples with atomic number 1-20.
questions.answer: Unreactive noble gases. Examples: Helium (He), Neon (Ne), Argon (Ar)
